question: The scatter plot shows CO₂ emissions vs. temperature anomaly. Which statement is best supported by the chart data?

options.A: There is a generally positive relationship between CO₂ emissions and temperature anomaly.
options.B: CO₂ emissions above 150 on the index always correspond to negative temperature anomalies.
options.C: There is no consistent relationship between CO₂ emissions and temperature anomaly.
options.D: Temperature anomalies are highest when CO₂ emissions are lowest.

explanation: This scatter plot uses dual encoding: point position shows the relationship, but point size and color also encode the y-variable, making high values overly salient. That can tempt viewers to overstate the strength or form of the relationship.
